channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: Earth’s Spin Is Slowing at a Pace Not Seen in Millions of Years—and You Can Guess Why - Gizmodo
item.description:
1. Earth’s Spin Is Slowing at a Pace Not Seen in Millions of Years—and You Can Guess Why  Gizmodo
  2. Earth’s days are getting longer. Climate change is to blame  Yahoo
  3. Rising sea levels are lengthening Earth’s days at an unprecedented rate  R&D World
  4. Researchers warn that global warming is altering the planet's rotation: with the melting of the polar ice caps and the redistribution of water in the oceans, the days are getting longer at a rate unseen for at least 3,6 million years.  CPG Click Petróleo e Gás
  5. ‘Unprecedented in the past 3.6 million years’: How human-made climate change is making days longer  Euronews.com

item.title: 'Interstellar messenger' 3I/ATLAS could be nearly as old as the universe itself, James Webb telescope reveals - Live Science
item.description:

item.description:

  4. The Sun’s galactic migration may have made life on Earth possible  Astronomy Magazine
  5. The Sun Was Formed 10,000 Light-Years Closer to the Milky Way Center. It Escaped in a Massive Migration of Thousands of Solar Twins  ZME Science
